zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Beauchamp於1335年出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick22歲，母親Katherine Mortimer22歲。她於大約1353年與Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1403年2月28日享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Beauchamp于1335年出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick22岁，母亲Katherine Mortimer22岁。她于大约1353年与Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1403年2月28日享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Beauchamp was born in 1335, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, was 22 and her mother, Katherine Mortimer, was 22. She married Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford about 1353, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 28 February 1403, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Beauchamp est née en 1335, à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Katherine Mortimer, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford environ 1353, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 février 1403, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Beauchamp 1335 in Warwick, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick 22 und ihre Mutter Katherine Mortimer 22. Sie hat um 1353 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Februar 1403 im Alter von 68 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1335 nel Warwick, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Katherine Mortimer, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford circa 1353, nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 28 febbraio 1403, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Beauchampが1335年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwickは22歳，母Katherine Mortimerは22歳であった。約1353年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルでRoger Clifford 2nd Baron Cliffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1403年2月28日に68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Beauchamp(이)가 1335년 영국 워윅실르 워윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Mortimer(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1353년 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1403년 2월 28일 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Beauchamp nasceu em 1335, em Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Mortimer, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford aproximadamente 1353, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1403, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Beauchamp родилась в 1335 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine Mortimer, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford около 1353 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 28 февраля 1403 г. в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Beauchamp nació en 1335, en Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Katherine Mortimer, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford aproximadamente 1353, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1403, a la edad de 68 años.
